description: ROUND 1 - The Contract Problem: Create a formal output format contract that tells the AI exactly how to communicate back, and add contract validation to the runner. Currently BOOT.md tells the AI to execute tasks but never specifies the required output format (TRANSITION: xxx, file operations, error reporting). The AI has no way to know what to output.
steps: Create kernel/contracts/output_format.md with formal specification: (1) TRANSITION: <condition> line format required in output, (2) FILES_WRITTEN: <path1>, <path2> for reporting file ops, (3) ERROR: <message> for errors, (4) STATUS: success|failure for overall result. Include examples for each node type.

Create kernel/contracts/__init__.py with OutputContractValidator class that has: validate_output(output: str, node_id: str) -> ContractResult with fields (valid: bool, transition: str|None, files_written: list[str], errors: list[str], status: str, violations: list[str])

Update kernel/prompts/planner.md to add a section referencing the output format contract: 'Your output MUST conform to kernel/contracts/output_format.md' and show the TRANSITION line examples specific to planner (plan_ready, plan_needs_revision)
Update kernel/prompts/coder.md similarly with coder-specific transitions (code_written, code_needs_retry)
Update kernel/prompts/tester.md similarly with tester transitions (tests_pass, tests_fail)
Update kernel/prompts/reviewer.md similarly with reviewer transitions (review_pass, review_needs_changes)
Update kernel/prompts/reflector.md similarly with reflector transitions (evolution_proposed, no_evolution_needed)
Update kernel/prompts/orchestrator.md similarly with orchestrator transitions (goal_loaded)
Update BOOT.md Step 6 to add: 'Your output MUST include a TRANSITION: <condition> line. See kernel/contracts/output_format.md for the full output format specification.'

Integrate OutputContractValidator into runner.py Mode 3: after parsing AI output, validate it against the contract before advancing. If invalid, log the violations as errors and stay on the same node.
Update context_assembler.py to include the output_format.md content in assembled prompts (new section === OUTPUT FORMAT CONTRACT ===)

Write tests in tests/test_contracts.py covering: valid outputs parse correctly, missing TRANSITION is a violation, invalid transition values for specific nodes, FILES_WRITTEN parsing, ERROR parsing, STATUS parsing, validator integration with runner
acceptance_criteria: kernel/contracts/output_format.md exists with clear format specification
kernel/contracts/__init__.py has OutputContractValidator class with validate_output method
All 6 prompts reference the output format contract with node-specific TRANSITION examples
BOOT.md mentions the output format requirement
context_assembler.py includes contract content in assembled output
runner.py Mode 3 validates output before advancing (invalid output stays on same node)
tests/test_contracts.py tests pass with comprehensive coverage
findings: Updated 6 existing tests in test_runner.py that tested fallback behavior (no TRANSITION or unmatched TRANSITION). These now test contract violation behavior instead. The old fallback-to-first-transition behavior is now replaced by contract validation that stays on the same node when output is non-compliant. Coverage remains at 99.02% with 317 tests passing.
